# nuxt-directus-sdk

> A Nuxt module for Directus with built-in authentication, realtime, file management, type generation, and visual editor support.

- GitHub: https://github.com/rolleyio/nuxt-directus-sdk
- Docs: https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io

## Overview

Production-ready Nuxt module for Directus CMS. Handles session-based authentication, realtime WebSocket subscriptions, file management, type generation from the Directus schema, visual editor integration, and permissions/rules sync. Sensible defaults out of the box; opt-in advanced configuration when you need it.

## Guides

- [Getting Started](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/getting-started) ‚Äî Installation, Directus backend configuration, next steps
- [Authentication](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/authentication) ‚Äî Session-based auth, SSO/OAuth, protected routes, user management
- [Realtime & WebSockets](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/realtime) ‚Äî Live subscriptions, auth modes, dev proxy
- [File Management](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/files) ‚Äî Uploads, transformations, `@nuxt/image` integration
- [Visual Editor](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/visual-editor) ‚Äî `DirectusVisualEditor` component, iframe detection, preview mode
- [Type Generation](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/type-generation) ‚Äî Auto-generated types, prefix, filtering with `include` / `exclude`
- [Dev Proxy](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/dev-proxy) ‚Äî CORS-free local development, split URLs for Docker/K8s
- [Server-Side Utilities](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/server-side) ‚Äî Server routes, admin operations, token auth

## Rules DSL

- [Defining Rules](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules) ‚Äî Code-first permissions and policies
- [Testing Rules](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules-testing) ‚Äî Unit-test access-control decisions
- [Sync & CLI](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/rules-sync) ‚Äî Pull, diff, and push rules

## CLI

- [Overview](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli) ‚Äî Connection settings, exit codes
- [`generate-types`](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli/generate-types) ‚Äî Generate TypeScript types from a Directus schema
- [`rules:*`](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/cli/rules) ‚Äî Pull, push, and diff Directus permissions/policies/roles

## API Reference

- [Module Options](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/module) ‚Äî `directus.*` configuration options
- [Environment Variables](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/env) ‚Äî `DIRECTUS_URL`, `DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN`, etc.
- [Directus Server Configuration](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/configuration/server) ‚Äî CORS, cookies, and other backend settings needed for cross-domain setups
- [Composables](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/composables) ‚Äî `useDirectus`, `useDirectusAuth`, `useDirectusStorage`, file helpers

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add -D nuxt-directus-sdk
```

```typescript
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['nuxt-directus-sdk'],
  directus: {
    url: process.env.DIRECTUS_URL,
  },
})
```

```dotenv
# .env
DIRECTUS_URL=https://your-directus.com
DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN=your-admin-token
```

## Requirements

- Nuxt 4.0+
- Directus v11.16.0+ (required by `@directus/visual-editing` v2 and `@directus/sdk` v21)

## SDK access

The module auto-imports every function exported by `@directus/sdk` at build time, so you can call `readItems`, `createItem`, `aggregate`, `customEndpoint`, etc. directly without importing. A small exclusion list covers client construction (`createDirectus`, `rest`, `realtime`, `staticToken`, `authentication`), auth internals (`auth`, `getAuthEndpoint`, `memoryStorage`), and GraphQL (`graphql`, `readGraphqlSdl`) ‚Äî see the [Composables page](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/api/composables#functions-excluded-from-auto-import) for why each is excluded and how to import them manually when needed.

The auto-import set tracks whichever version of `@directus/sdk` the user has installed, so new SDK functions are available automatically on upgrade. Disable or customise via `directus.autoImportSdk` in config.

## Type generation

Types are generated from the Directus schema at build time and made available globally (`DirectusSchema`, `CollectionNames`, one interface per collection). Enabled by default when `DIRECTUS_ADMIN_TOKEN` is set.

**Inside Nuxt apps the generated types are global ‚Äî do NOT add `import type { DirectusSchema } from '#build/...'` (or anywhere else) when authoring rules, schemas, or composables.** The generated `.d.ts` is wrapped in `declare global { ... }` and Nuxt picks it up automatically. Just reference `DirectusSchema`, `CollectionNames`, or any collection interface directly.

For standalone scripts that run outside the Nuxt build (`bun server/scripts/foo.ts`, plain Node scripts, CI tasks), the global declaration isn't visible. Use the standalone CLI to emit a regular `.d.ts` and reference it from the script:

```bash
npx nuxt-directus-sdk generate-types -o types/directus.d.ts
```

```typescript
/// <reference path="../types/directus.d.ts" />
import { defineDirectusRules } from 'nuxt-directus-sdk/rules'

const rules = defineDirectusRules<DirectusSchema>({ ... })
```

Options: `prefix` to namespace types (applies to custom interfaces *and* to `DirectusSchema` / `CollectionNames` ‚Äî most projects should leave it unset), `include` / `exclude` to narrow what's emitted (references to missing collections collapse to `string` / `string[]`), `expandReferences` to auto-pull referenced collections when using `include`, and `verbose` for per-reference rewrite logs. See the [Type Generation guide](https://nuxt-directus-sdk.rolley.io/guide/type-generation) for the full story.

## Breaking changes in 6.0

- `@directus/sdk` peer dep bumped to `>=21`
- `@directus/types` peer dep bumped to `>=15`
- `@directus/visual-editing` peer dep bumped to `>=2.0` (requires Directus v11.16.0+)
- `DirectusAddButton` and `DirectusEditButton` components removed; use `useDirectusVisualEditor()` or `@directus/visual-editing`'s `apply()` directly for custom UI
- `--prefix` (and `types.prefix`) now also prefixes the schema interface and names enum: `App` produces `AppDirectusSchema` and `AppCollectionNames` (previously only custom collection interfaces were prefixed). Most projects don't use a prefix; if you do, search-and-replace `DirectusSchema` ‚Üí `<Prefix>DirectusSchema` and `CollectionNames` ‚Üí `<Prefix>CollectionNames` in your code. The recommended path for new projects is to leave `--prefix` unset.
